info.title: CCDI Federation Service
info.description: REST API for querying CCDI-DCC  graph database

get.description: Get a paginated list of subjects with optional filtering

parameters.name: sex

schema.description: Matches any subject where the `sex` field matches the string provided.
schema.title: Sex

parameters.description: Matches any subject where the `sex` field matches the string provided.

parameters.name: race

schema.description: Matches any subject where any member of the `race` field matches any of the provided values. Multiple race values can be provided separated by `||` (double pipe). The race field in the database may contain semicolon-separated values (e.g., 'Asian;White'), and the filter will match if any of the provided values is found within those values. Only `||` is accepted as a delimiter; all other characters are treated as part of a single value.
enum: Native Hawaiian or other Pacific Islander
schema.title: Race

parameters.description: Matches any subject where any member of the `race` field matches any of the provided values. Multiple race values can be provided separated by `||` (double pipe). The race field in the database may contain semicolon-separated values (e.g., 'Asian;White'), and the filter will match if any of the provided values is found within those values. Only `||` is accepted as a delimiter; all other characters are treated as part of a single value.

parameters.name: ethnicity

schema.description: Matches any subject where the `ethnicity` field matches the string provided. Ethnicity is derived from race values: if race contains 'Hispanic or Latino', ethnicity is 'Hispanic or Latino'; otherwise 'Not reported'. Only these two values are accepted.
schema.title: Ethnicity

parameters.description: Matches any subject where the `ethnicity` field matches the string provided. Ethnicity is derived from race values: if race contains 'Hispanic or Latino', ethnicity is 'Hispanic or Latino'; otherwise 'Not reported'. Only these two values are accepted.

parameters.name: identifiers

schema.description: Matches any subject where any member of the `identifiers` field matches the string provided. **Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the subject should be included in the results.
schema.title: Identifiers

parameters.description: Matches any subject where any member of the `identifiers` field matches the string provided. **Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the subject should be included in the results.

parameters.name: vital_status

schema.description: Matches any subject where the `vital_status` field matches the string provided.
schema.title: Vital Status

parameters.description: Matches any subject where the `vital_status` field matches the string provided.

parameters.name: age_at_vital_status

schema.description: Matches any subject where the `age_at_vital_status` field matches the string provided.
schema.title: Age At Vital Status

parameters.description: Matches any subject where the `age_at_vital_status` field matches the string provided.

parameters.name: depositions

schema.description: Filter by study_id. Matches any subject where the `depositions` field contains the specified study_id value (e.g., `phs002431`). Returns all participants that belong to the specified study. Example: `depositions=phs002431` will return all participants in study `phs002431`.
schema.title: Depositions

parameters.description: Filter by study_id. Matches any subject where the `depositions` field contains the specified study_id value (e.g., `phs002431`). Returns all participants that belong to the specified study. Example: `depositions=phs002431` will return all participants in study `phs002431`.

parameters.name: associated_diagnosis_categories

schema.description: Matches any subject where a diagnosis node's `diagnosis_category` matches the value (case-insensitive token after `;` split). Harmonized (CDE 16607972) or unharmonized values. Aligned with CCDI Federation API aggregation subject filtering (v1.3+).
schema.title: Associated Diagnosis Categories

parameters.description: Matches any subject where a diagnosis node's `diagnosis_category` matches the value (case-insensitive token after `;` split). Harmonized (CDE 16607972) or unharmonized values. Aligned with CCDI Federation API aggregation subject filtering (v1.3+).

parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page index. Defaults to 1.
schema.title: Page

parameters.description: 1-based page index. Defaults to 1.

parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 50. Maximum: 500. When any filter query parameter is present and per_page exceeds 500, per_page is silently reduced to 50.

parameters.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 50. Maximum: 500. When any filter query parameter is present and per_page exceeds 500, per_page is silently reduced to 50.

get.summary: Reports summary information for the subjects known by this server.
get.description: Returns the total count of all subjects. This endpoint does not accept any query parameters.

get.summary: Gets the samples known by this server.
get.description:
Gets the samples known by this server.

### Pagination

This endpoint is paginated. Users may override the default pagination
parameters by providing one or more of the pagination-related query
parameters below.

### Filtering

All harmonized (top-level) and unharmonized (nested under the
`metadata.unharmonized` key) metadata fields are filterable. To achieve
this, you can provide the field name as a [`String`]. Filtering follows the
following rules:

* For single-value metadata field, the sample is included in the results if
its value _exactly_ matches the query string. Matches are case-sensitive.
* For multiple-value metadata fields, the sample is included in the results
if any of its values for the field _exactly_ match the query string (a
logical OR (`||`)). Matches are case-sensitive.
* When the metadata field is `null` (in the case of singular or
multiple-valued metadata fields) or empty, the sample is not included.
* When multiple fields are provided as filters, a logical AND (`&&`) strings
together the predicates. In other words, all filters must match for a
sample to be returned. Note that this means that servers do not natively
support logical OR (`||`) across multiple fields: that must be done by
calling this endpoint with each of your desired queries and performing a
set union of those samples out of band.

### Ordering

This endpoint has default ordering requirements—those details are documented
in the `responses::Samples` schema.

get.summary: Experimental: Filter the samples known by this server by free-text diagnosis search.
get.description:
Experimental: Filter the samples known by this server by free-text diagnosis search.

### Diagnosis Filtering

This endpoint supports the experimental `search` parameter: case-insensitive substring on
`diagnosis` from diagnosis nodes, or on `diagnosis_comment` when `diagnosis` is
`see diagnosis_comment`.

The `diagnosis_category` query parameter matches the **full** `diagnosis_category` string
with a case-insensitive **substring**. With `search`, both apply as **AND** on the same
diagnosis node.

### Pagination

This endpoint is paginated. Users may override the default pagination
parameters by providing one or more of the pagination-related query
parameters below.

### Additional Filtering

All harmonized (top-level) and unharmonized (nested under the
`metadata.unharmonized` key) metadata fields are filterable. To achieve
this, you can provide the field name as a [`String`]. Filtering follows the
following rules:

* For single-value metadata field, the sample is included in the results if
its value _exactly_ matches the query string. Matches are case-sensitive.
* For multiple-value metadata fields, the sample is included in the results
if any of its values for the field _exactly_ match the query string (a
logical OR [`||`]). Matches are case-sensitive.
* When the metadata field is `null` (in the case of singular or
multiple-valued metadata fields) or empty, the sample is not included.
* When multiple fields are provided as filters, a logical AND (`&&`) strings
together the predicates. In other words, all filters must match for a
sample to be returned. Note that this means that servers do not natively
support logical OR (`||`) across multiple fields: that must be done by
calling this endpoint with each of your desired queries and performing a
set union of those samples out of band.

### Ordering

This endpoint has default ordering requirements—those details are documented
in the `responses::Samples` schema.

Note: This API is experimental and is subject to change without being considered
as a breaking change.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match on diagnosis text from diagnosis nodes (`diagnosis` property). When `diagnosis` is the sentinel value `see diagnosis_comment`, the search is applied to `diagnosis_comment` instead. May be combined with `diagnosis_category` (AND on the same diagnosis node).

get.summary: Experimental: Filter the subjects known by this server by free-text diagnosis search.
get.description:
Experimental: filter subjects using diagnosis nodes (`diagnosis`, `diagnosis_category`)
alongside the usual `GET /subject` query parameters. Supports harmonized and unharmonized
diagnosis category values at the data node, in line with the
[CCDI Federation API aggregation](https://cbiit.github.io/ccdi-federation-api-aggregation/) subject model (v1.3+).

`search`: case-insensitive substring on `diagnosis` (or `diagnosis_comment` when diagnosis is `see diagnosis_comment`).
`associated_diagnosis_categories`: substring on the full `diagnosis_category` on diagnosis node. Both together apply as AND on one diagnosis node.

Paginated. Experimental—may change.

parameters.name: search

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring match on diagnosis text from diagnosis nodes (`diagnosis` property). When `diagnosis` is the sentinel value `see diagnosis_comment`, the search is applied to `diagnosis_comment` instead (same behavior as sample diagnosis search). May be combined with `associated_diagnosis_categories` (AND on the same diagnosis node).
schema.title: Search

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring match on diagnosis text from diagnosis nodes (`diagnosis` property). When `diagnosis` is the sentinel value `see diagnosis_comment`, the search is applied to `diagnosis_comment` instead (same behavior as sample diagnosis search). May be combined with `associated_diagnosis_categories` (AND on the same diagnosis node).

parameters.name: associated_diagnosis_categories

schema.description: Case-insensitive substring on the full `diagnosis_category` on a diagnosis node (harmonized or unharmonized text). With `search`, AND on the same node.
schema.title: Associated Diagnosis Categories

parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring on the full `diagnosis_category` on a diagnosis node (harmonized or unharmonized text). With `search`, AND on the same node.

Only `||` is accepted as a delimiter; all other characters are treated as part of a single value.

get.summary: Gets files known by this server (methylation array and sequencing).
get.description:
Gets files known by this server.

Results include files from **methylation_array_file** and **sequencing_file** graph node types, returned in a single merged `File` shape.

### Pagination

This endpoint is paginated. Users may override the default pagination
parameters by providing one or more of the pagination-related query
parameters below.

### Filtering

All harmonized (top-level) and unharmonized (nested under the
`metadata.unharmonized` key) metadata fields are filterable. To achieve
this, you can provide the field name as a [`String`]. Filtering follows the
following rules:

* For single-value metadata field, the file is included in the results if
its value _exactly_ matches the query string. Matches are case-sensitive.
* For multiple-value metadata fields, the file is included in the results
if any of its values for the field _exactly_ match the query string (a
logical OR (`||`)). Matches are case-sensitive.
* When the metadata field is `null` (in the case of singular or
multiple-valued metadata fields) or empty, the file is not included.
* When multiple fields are provided as filters, a logical AND (`&&`) strings
together the predicates. In other words, all filters must match for a
file to be returned. Note that this means that servers do not natively
support logical OR (`||`) across multiple fields: that must be done by
calling this endpoint with each of your desired queries and performing a
set union of those files out of band.

### Ordering

Paginated results are ordered by node type (**methylation_array_file** first, then
**sequencing_file**), then by file id within each type. Further details are documented
in the `responses::Files` schema.

parameters.name: type

schema.description: Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_type` field matches the string provided.
schema.title: Type

parameters.description: Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_type` field matches the string provided.

parameters.name: size

schema.description: Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_size` field matches the string provided.
schema.title: Size

parameters.description: Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_size` field matches the string provided.

parameters.name: checksums

schema.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `md5sum` or `checksum_value` field matches the string provided.

**Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the file should be included in the results.
schema.title: Checksums

parameters.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `md5sum` or `checksum_value` field matches the string provided.

**Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the file should be included in the results.

parameters.name: description

schema.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_description` field matches the string provided.

**Note:** a file is returned if the value provided is a substring of the description.
schema.title: Description

parameters.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where the `file_description` field matches the string provided.

**Note:** a file is returned if the value provided is a substring of the description.

parameters.name: depositions

schema.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where any member of the `depositions` fields match the string provided.

**Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the file should be included in the results.
schema.title: Depositions

parameters.description:
Matches any file (methylation_array_file or sequencing_file) where any member of the `depositions` fields match the string provided.

**Note:** a logical OR (`||`) is performed across the values when determining whether the file should be included in the results.

parameters.name: metadata.unharmonized.file_name

schema.description:
All unharmonized fields should be filterable in the same manner as harmonized fields:

* Filtering on a singular field should include the `File` in the results if the query exactly matches the value of that field for the `File` (case-sensitive).
* Filtering on field with multiple values should include the `File` in the results if the query exactly matches any of the values of the field for that `File` (case-sensitive).
* Unlike harmonized fields, unharmonized fields must be prefixed with `metadata.unharmonized`.

**Examples:**
* `?metadata.unharmonized.file_name=UTYE.fastq` - Filter files by file_name
schema.title: Metadata.Unharmonized.File Name

parameters.description:
All unharmonized fields should be filterable in the same manner as harmonized fields:

* Filtering on a singular field should include the `File` in the results if the query exactly matches the value of that field for the `File` (case-sensitive).
* Filtering on field with multiple values should include the `File` in the results if the query exactly matches any of the values of the field for that `File` (case-sensitive).
* Unlike harmonized fields, unharmonized fields must be prefixed with `metadata.unharmonized`.

**Examples:**
* `?metadata.unharmonized.file_name=UTYE.fastq` - Filter files by file_name

get.summary: Groups files by metadata field and returns counts (methylation array and sequencing).
get.description: Groups files by the specified metadata field and returns counts. Results include files from **methylation_array_file** and **sequencing_file** graph node types, returned in a single merged `File` shape. Only 'type' and 'depositions' are supported.

parameters.name: field

schema.description: The field to group by and count. Only 'type' and 'depositions' are supported.

get.summary: Gets a file by identifier (methylation array or sequencing).
get.description: Gets the file matching the provided identifier (if it exists). Results include files from **methylation_array_file** and **sequencing_file** graph node types, returned in a single merged `File` shape. Organization must be 'CCDI-DCC'. Namespace is the study_id value from the database. Name is the file id field value from the database.

schema.title: Response Get Organizations Api V1 Organization Get

get.description:
Returns the organization matching the provided name (if it exists).

Returns 404 with error response if the organization is not found.
The name can match either the identifier or the name field.

This endpoint provides example error responses for different error types.
    This is a reference endpoint showing the structure of error responses
    that may be returned by other endpoints.
    
    **Error Types:**
    - `InvalidRoute`: Returned when requesting a non-existent route
    - `InvalidParameters`: Returned when request parameters are invalid
    - `NotFound`: Returned when a requested resource is not found
    - `UnshareableData`: Returned when data cannot be shared due to agreements
    - `UnsupportedField`: Returned when a field is not supported for operations
    
    For 400 or 404 errors, always use a customized response to avoid exposing
    any internal error messages or user inputs. Any error not covered by the
    categories below should return a 404 NotFound error.

ErrorDetail.description:
Individual error detail model matching OpenAPI specification.

Fields vary by error kind:
- InvalidRoute: kind, method, route, message
- InvalidParameters: kind, parameters, message, reason
- NotFound: kind, entity, message, reason
- UnshareableData: kind, entity, message, reason
- UnsupportedField: kind, field, message, reason
